ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Standard code used to represent the country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region to which the country belongs */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Name of the capital city of the country */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Total area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Official currency of the country */,
  "official_language" TEXT /* Official language of the country */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the country (people per square kilometer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_stats" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population statistic entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country for which the statistics apply */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the statistics are recorded */,
  "total_population" REAL /* Total population of the country for that year */,
  "urban_population_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the population living in urban areas */,
  "rural_population_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the population living in rural areas */,
  "population_growth_rate" REAL /* Annual population growth rate */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population */,
  "life_expectancy" REAL /* Average life expectancy at birth */,
  "fertility_rate" REAL /* Total fertility rate */,
  "sex_ratio" REAL /* Sex ratio (males per 100 females) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_stats_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "countries" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of associated country codes */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent to which the region belongs */,
  "population" REAL /* Total population of the region */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Total area of the region in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the system */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or organization of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User account status (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who accessed the data */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population statistic entry that was accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access performed (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used to access the data */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES population_stats ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version of the population data */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population statistic entry */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the data */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was updated */,
  "updated_by_user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the update */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the changes made in this version */,
  "approved_by_user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who approved the version */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was approved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_approved_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_updated_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES population_stats ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population statistic entry */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Assessment of the data quality */,
  "data_source_url" TEXT /* URL of the source from which the data was collected */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the metadata was last updated */,
  "confidence_level" TEXT /* Confidence level of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metadata_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES population_stats ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visualizations" (
  "viz_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the population statistic entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who created the visualization */,
  "viz_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., chart, map) */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the visualization was created */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path where the visualization is stored */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the visualization */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of tags for categorizing the visualization */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the visualization has been viewed */,
  "last_viewed_date" TEXT /* Date when the visualization was last viewed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("viz_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_visualizations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_visualizations_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES population_stats ("stat_id")
)
